122486.explanation: Diversity is important, and the municipality should set a good example. The top 10 on our candidate list includes an equal number of men and women. This is a shared task for the new college to be formed.

122487.explanation: Residents must have a say in municipal policy. A referendum is not the right tool for this. A simple yes or no does not do justice to nuances and the full opinion of residents. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers better participation to better represent residents.

122490.explanation: The municipality must help farmers who want to earn money in ways other than agriculture, but under conditions: landscape values must also be protected by the municipality. The municipality should approach farmers' initiatives with an open attitude.

122493.explanation: The municipality of Rheden must also contribute to the reception of asylum seekers. Rheden has always been a hospitable municipality. With already planned reception locations in the new neighborhoods, Rheden is already doing its part.

122494.explanation: Employers must ensure the welfare of employees, so good housing is a must. At the same time, dependency of foreign workers on their employer must be reduced. There must therefore be more housing options than just at the company.

122496.explanation: The municipality must stand for clean air. Wood burning causes health problems and restricts the living space of people with lung issues. Therefore, we need a burning policy that respects residents' health and the freedom of individuals who want to burn wood.

122498.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to organize fireworks shows. However, the municipality could support initiatives for fireworks shows from the community.

122500.explanation: Accessibility is very important for people with disabilities. Many places are still not properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations. Therefore, we believe the municipality should help.

122506.explanation: The municipality must ensure a varied sports offering in the municipality. This means the municipality must provide sports parks and sports halls. When an association is financially struggling, the municipality should determine support based on the individual situation.

122508.explanation: Safety is primarily the task of the police. GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to invest in prevention and livability by spending money on poverty reduction, housing, youth workers, and maintaining facilities. This contributes more structurally to safety.

122509.explanation: Camera surveillance is not a solution in itself. GroenLinks-PvdA only supports camera surveillance under strict conditions. This prevents false security and privacy violations.

122511.explanation: We are in a housing crisis. It is especially hard for first-time buyers to find a home. Therefore, the municipality must take measures in the form of a loan. The structural solution lies in building more homes, as is underway in Haveland and Velp South.

122512.explanation: The municipality must ensure everyone can find housing. People who grew up in a village or the region already have ties to the village. Therefore, they should have priority. The municipality must primarily build according to residents' needs.

122517.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for everyone being able to participate in society. People with low incomes deserve extra support from the municipality. We believe those with the greatest ability to pay should bear the heaviest burdens.

122518.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA stands for everyone being able to participate in society. People with low incomes deserve extra support from the municipality. Extra investment ensures more stability in low-income households, enabling them to contribute more to society and independently escape poverty.

122520.explanation: The municipality wants to be CO2-neutral by 2040. Electric cars contribute to this. The municipality must stimulate the transition by arranging charging points for electric cars. These charging points can also be used to prevent grid congestion on the electricity network.

122521.explanation: Villages must be as safe as possible. There must be space for pedestrians and cyclists. To allow traffic participants to share space as safely as possible, a speed limit of 30 km/h in built-up areas is better.

122522.explanation: Healthcare providers aim to optimally promote the health and well-being of their clients. They do not discriminate based on the client's faith, culture, or beliefs, but they remain mindful of these aspects. Clients should therefore not have to make an individual choice in this regard.

122523.explanation: Care must be well delivered, especially for vulnerable groups such as young people. Good support ensures that young people can fully participate in society and helps prevent problems at an early stage. Good youth care empowers young people and helps them find their place in society.

122524.explanation: Our municipality has a property tax (OZB) that is below the Gelderland average. The focus should be on providing proper care, preserving the landscape, and maintaining facilities. The level of the OZB should not be an end in itself.

122525.explanation: The municipal dog tax is spent on amenities for dog owners, such as dog parks and trash bins. This is the main purpose of the dog tax. If we abolish the dog tax, we can no longer guarantee these facilities.

122526.explanation: Like residents, tourists use our municipality's facilities. They also enjoy the landscape that we maintain. Therefore, it is reasonable to request a contribution from them.

122529.explanation: Refugees have not been able to accumulate waiting time, so to ensure a fair chance at housing, priority is necessary. Priority in social housing is needed for rapid integration: housing enables language learning, work, and education.

122530.explanation: Processing waste costs money, and users should contribute to this. However, renewable raw materials can be delivered for free. This encourages better waste separation and more reuse.

122532.explanation: Every child must learn to swim. The Netherlands is a water-rich country. The danger posed by water is everywhere, so swimming lessons are very important. These lessons do not necessarily have to be through school swimming but can also be supported by providing funding for swimming lessons.

122533.explanation: Soft drugs are permitted in the Netherlands, and there is no reason to specifically prohibit them in our municipality. If there are plans for a coffee shop in Rheden, these should be reviewed like any other plans. The municipality can, however, emphasize prevention and health.

122534.explanation: Our municipality mainly needs affordable housing. This applies to both social rental and low-cost home ownership. Building a large portion of new social rental homes also ensures more space for mid-priced homes in other neighborhoods. This makes the housing stock more diverse.

122535.explanation: Our landscape is a core quality. At the same time, we see that the housing shortage is high. GroenLinks-PvdA chooses to build in village centers, including high-rise buildings.

122537.explanation: In a merger, the resident must remain central. We believe that through a merger the municipality can provide better services and gain more influence in the region and The Hague. However, we must establish the correct preconditions before proceeding with a merger.

122539.explanation: To make room in our municipality for housing and to ensure jobs, we must optimally utilize our industrial zones. This means we choose to use industrial areas for companies that contribute to sustainability and have many employees.

122486.explanation: Diversity leads to better decision-making and a municipality that is recognizable to everyone. D66 considers a good gender balance in the council very desirable. Other forms of diversity in the council, such as background, age, and residence of council members, are also important.

122487.explanation: In principle, municipal councillors, elected by residents, have the task of incorporating residents' opinions in decisions about important issues. D66 believes that residents, with sufficient signatures, should be allowed to request a corrective binding referendum.

122490.explanation: 'Always' is a strong word; there must be alignment with goals such as nature conservation, and local residents should be able to contribute to the planning. But D66 favors a 'yes, provided that' approach to initiatives rather than 'no, unless'.

122493.explanation: There are already asylum seeker centers in the municipality of Rheden. To also structurally provide accommodation for asylum seekers, two new residential neighborhoods will be developed with approximately 70-80 homes for asylum seekers, who will be housed in 'regular houses' in the neighborhood with proper support. D66 fully supports this choice.

122494.explanation: In the municipality of Rheden, there are already more than enough homes available for labor migrants, but only some work in our municipality. Entrepreneurs in agriculture and horticulture do not always have the proper housing facilities on their own industrial sites.

122496.explanation: Wood burning can harm air quality, cause health damage, and cause nuisance. D66 chooses clear rules that significantly limit pollution and nuisance without unnecessarily burdening people who already burn cleanly. Modern wood stoves remain allowed, open fireplaces do not.

122498.explanation: A good, professional fireworks show costs about as much as the annual budget for a village hall. For D66, organizing fireworks shows with municipal money in various villages is not a priority.

122500.explanation: D66 considers it very important that facilities, buildings, and digital services are accessible to everyone. As a municipality, we must ensure that village centers are well accessible for people with disabilities. This is a priority for us over subsidizing entrepreneurs.

122506.explanation: D66 Rheden sees sports and exercise as essential for health, well-being, and social connection. It is the municipality's task to ensure there are sufficient and affordable sports facilities in the municipality so that a variety of sports can continue to be practiced here.

122508.explanation: Boe's (enforcement officers) have the important task of enforcing rules and intervening in unsafe situations that occur daily. Currently, there is insufficient evidence to claim that we have too few enforcement officers, but if research shows this, we will support increasing capacity.

122509.explanation: Respecting everyone’s privacy is an important principle for D66 Rheden. We do not want cameras that record public spaces unless there is a necessity from a safety perspective.

122511.explanation: D66 Rheden wants an inclusive housing market where housing is seen as a social fundamental right. There must also be sufficient opportunities for first-time buyers to purchase suitable homes. A starter's loan makes this possible, but we must also build more affordable owner-occupied homes for starters.

122512.explanation: D66 supports the (already existing) rule that 50% of vacant social rental homes are allocated to housing seekers from within the municipality.

122517.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes is one of the measures through which the municipality helps low-income residents to make ends meet. We do not want to take this away from those in need.

122518.explanation: The current income limits for the various minimum arrangements ensure that aid reaches those residents who need it most. The municipality of Rheden applies a higher income limit for assistance to families with children; in our view, this should remain so.

122520.explanation: The option to drive electric should not be available only to residents with a private driveway, so the number of public charging stations must grow, matched to demand. D66 also wants the municipality to better facilitate the arrival of shared electric cars.

122521.explanation: We primarily see residential streets and courtyards as places to play and stay, and secondarily for driving through and parking. Safety is paramount in traffic: inside built-up areas, the speed limit will be 30 kilometers per hour wherever possible.

122522.explanation: D66 Rheden highly values residents' freedom of choice. This also applies to the free choice of a healthcare provider who fits their faith, culture, or beliefs.

122523.explanation: The use and cost of youth care have exploded since decentralization. The solution is not cheaper procurement but preventing heavier care through prevention and early detection. For children who need youth care, it must be accessible and of high quality.

122524.explanation: The property tax (OZB) provides income needed, for example, to maintain village halls and the library. Lowering the OZB means fewer facilities. The current OZB is equal to the Gelderland average. Moreover, the OZB is a social tax: the strongest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens.

122525.explanation: The dog tax is a very inefficient tax; about one-third is spent on collection and enforcement costs. If there is more budget space, it is better to abolish this tax. As long as that is not possible, a fixed fee per dog should apply.

122526.explanation: Tourism and recreation are important for our economy. But tourists also use our facilities and infrastructure. It is only fair that tourists contribute appropriately through the tourist tax.

122529.explanation: Every municipality in the Netherlands, including ours, has a task to house recognized refugees. By allocating part of the vacant social rental homes to recognized refugees, we fulfill our responsibility to provide housing for them.

122530.explanation: 'Free' waste collection does not exist; you pay per kilo at the recycling center or through waste charges. A downside of paying per kilo is that it increases the chance of illegal dumping. D66 thinks it is a good idea to evaluate whether a higher amount of free kilos prevents illegal dumping.

122532.explanation: Although we would very much like to, the municipality does not have the financial resources to enable obtaining a swimming diploma within primary education. D66 does want to offer schools the possibility to use the municipality's swimming pools for free.

122533.explanation: Currently, it is not on the agenda, but D66 Rheden has no principled objections to establishing a coffee shop in our municipality. We do support the 'distance criterion': the minimum distance between a coffee shop and a school must be 250 meters.

122534.explanation: D66 finds it important that we build for all housing seekers. There is too little social rental housing, but also too little supply for starters and those looking to move within home ownership. With the current norm of 30% social rental, a new construction project is more profitable and therefore more viable.

122535.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage in the municipality of Rheden, and we must use our space optimally. Therefore, D66 supports high-rise buildings that suit the location of a new construction project. In some places, a tall residential building fits better than in others.

122537.explanation: The municipality of Brummen and the municipality of Rheden have similar identities and similar priorities. By forming a new municipality together, the interests of residents can be better served in the long term.

122539.explanation: The model building club EVMC has been located at the Beemd for 40 years with a facility unique in the region. Relocation is not a realistic option. The club is willing to pay a market-based price for their current property. The economic benefit to the municipality of one additional company in this location is very limited.

122486.explanation: The VVD chooses quality and suitability. Therefore, we appoint aldermen based on experience, expertise, and support, not based on a fixed quota.

122487.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality should simply work well and always engage in dialogue with residents. In Rheden, this means that we make and execute decisions so that residents do not have to worry about them constantly.

122490.explanation: The VVD wants to hinder farmers and entrepreneurs as little as possible. If a farmer wants to run a business such as a shop or campground, this should basically be possible.

122493.explanation: The VVD believes we should do what is legally required, but no more than that. Our municipality already accommodates more people than necessary according to the distribution law. If too many people are accommodated, we close locations.

122494.explanation: The VVD believes employers should take responsibility for their staff. Housing on or near the company can be a good solution, as long as it is done properly and fits the environment of our villages.

122496.explanation: The VVD believes responsible wood burning and cleaner air can go well together. Patronizing bans do not help: we choose personal responsibility and encouraging cleaner technologies.

122498.explanation: The VVD believes that now there is a national fireworks ban, the municipality should assist in organizing fireworks displays.

122500.explanation: The VVD finds it important that everyone can participate in our villages. Entrepreneurs have the responsibility to make their buildings accessible, and we as a municipality do this for the environment.

122506.explanation: The VVD considers associations the backbone of our villages. Together with associations, we first look at how they can better fulfill their own responsibilities.

122508.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to feel safe in our villages. Therefore, we want more visible public order officers on the streets who can intervene quickly in cases of nuisance and collaborate with the police.

122509.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to feel safe in our villages. We deploy CCTV surveillance targeted at places where it is needed to counteract nuisance and crime and to increase the feeling of safety.

122511.explanation: The VVD wants to realize more owner-occupied homes and more housing turnover. This so that first-time buyers have a fair chance at a home.

122512.explanation: The VVD believes that people from our villages should also be able to continue living in their own community. At the same time, we focus strongly on turnover so that those who have progressed can make the step to rental or ownership, and social housing becomes available for those who really need it.

122517.explanation: The VVD believes people who really need help should receive support. Custom solutions are necessary here. But locally, we do not create rules that reduce the gap between working and receiving benefits.

122518.explanation: The VVD believes aid should reach people who truly need it. We do not create local rules that reduce the difference between working and receiving benefits.

122520.explanation: The VVD wants to realize more charging points. This makes electric driving easier without compromising accessibility and parking in our villages.

122521.explanation: The VVD opts for 30 km/h zones where it truly improves safety.

122522.explanation: The VVD believes good care should be as person-centered as possible. At the same time, care must remain available, affordable, and of good quality, so we look at what is possible and appropriate in each situation.

122523.explanation: The VVD believes good youth care is about quality and results, not the lowest price. Poor or too cheap care costs more later and does not help children and families.

122524.explanation: The VVD wants to counter the waste of your tax money. This creates room to reduce the property tax (OZB).

122525.explanation: The VVD wants to counter the waste of your tax money. Furthermore, we believe the dog tax is outdated. Therefore, from 2027, the first dog will be free, and we will gradually abolish the dog tax.

122526.explanation: The VVD wants to keep our villages attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs. By keeping the tourist tax low, recreation remains affordable, and we strengthen hospitality, village centers, and employment.

122529.explanation: The VVD believes social rental housing is meant for people living here who urgently need a home. By giving priority to residents from our villages and strongly focusing on turnover, we keep social housing available for those who need it.

122530.explanation: The VVD wants to make it easy for residents to dispose of waste properly. By making the recycling center freely accessible, we prevent illegal dumping and ensure clean and tidy villages.

122532.explanation: The VVD believes parents are primarily responsible for swimming lessons. Where children risk being left out, we offer targeted support, but we do not choose a general municipal scheme for school swimming.

122533.explanation: The VVD always puts safety first. A coffee shop brings additional risks of nuisance and crime and does not fit in our villages.

122534.explanation: The VVD wants to build owner-occupied homes. That creates turnover and frees up social rental homes for those who really need them.

122535.explanation: The VVD wants to build to solve the housing shortage, mainly by realizing more owner-occupied homes and turnover. Sometimes that can be taller in logical places but always fitting our villages.

122537.explanation: The VVD is open to cooperation if it strengthens our villages. A merger is not a goal in itself: only if residents benefit and our identity is preserved can this be a good step.

122539.explanation: The VVD values associations and wants to give them space. At the same time, we want to use industrial estates for their intended purposes.

122486.explanation: Burgerbelangen believes that officials should be appointed based on their skills. We believe gender does not play a role in the quality of an official. We do not support quotas that consider only personal characteristics such as gender.

122487.explanation: Not every subject is suitable for a referendum. However, in principle, we are in favor of maximally including the voice of residents in decision-making. For some very fundamental and important issues, we think a referendum could be a good idea.

122490.explanation: We believe the municipality should support farmers to keep their businesses viable. Only then is it possible to offer local perspective to farmers. A shop or a campground are good methods that minimally burden the environment.

122493.explanation: Rheden already complies with the distribution law. With this, we take responsibility for the fair accommodation of asylum seekers. We are not in favor of more asylum seeker centers.

122494.explanation: What matters is that it is done decently and that our residents do not experience inconvenience. The municipality must make strict agreements with housing providers for labor migrants to ensure decent housing and intervene firmly in case of nuisance.

122496.explanation: Burgerbelangen opposes a ban on wood burning. Furthermore, we advocate for national policy.

122498.explanation: We do not see it as the municipality’s task to organize a fireworks show. Within the limits of the law, the municipality should provide space for residents, village associations, and others to organize something like this.

122500.explanation: The accessibility of buildings is primarily the responsibility of the building owner. Furthermore, Rheden simply does not have the funds to pay for this. Therefore, it is not even feasible.

122506.explanation: The municipality should not provide financial contributions to sports clubs. Of course, we can be flexible with, for example, hall rental fees.

122508.explanation: We find it important that rules can be enforced. However, increasing the number of municipal law enforcement officers must not come at the expense of the already limited police deployment.

122509.explanation: Only in places where nuisance is experienced that could be resolved by camera surveillance. If there is no nuisance, then no cameras should be installed.

122511.explanation: We want to make it easier for starters to buy a home. The first priority is to build more houses. This structurally solves the problem. In the short term, starter loans could help.

122512.explanation: We believe it is important that people can find housing in their own environment. This way, you can also maintain your network. We address our housing market regionally. So someone from Velp can just as easily live in Arnhem as vice versa. A social network does not adhere to municipal boundaries.

122517.explanation: By stopping debt cancellation, these residents would face financial problems. This would only make the problems bigger and increase social issues. So we gain nothing from it. It is better for these residents and the municipality as a whole not to stop the debt cancellation.

122518.explanation: Aid for poverty should only go to people who are actually in poverty. Raising the threshold means more people become entitled to something for which there is no necessity. This also costs the municipality a lot of money. Rheden does not have this. It is financially impossible to implement.

122520.explanation: Most parking spaces already have designated charging points for electric cars. Designating more spots for electric cars means fewer spots for other cars.

122521.explanation: In many places, this is already the case, and we will not change that. We oppose lowering the speed limit on the main roads that run through our villages. This would lead to longer response times for emergency services and possibly the disappearance of bus lines.

122522.explanation: Care is about quality, not religious belief. It can sometimes help if a care provider is familiar with certain cultures and has adapted their care accordingly, but ultimately, as a government, we are responsible for ensuring you receive good care.

122523.explanation: If you only look at price in care, then cheap ends up being expensive. We want youth care to be handled properly so that young people can eventually leave the care process. This may lead to costs in the short term but yields much more in the long term.

122524.explanation: At this moment, it is not affordable for Rheden to lower property tax (ozb). There is no money for this, so cuts would have to be made. We have already made tough cuts in recent years. A reduction is not possible now. If funds become available, we would certainly like to lower the ozb.

122525.explanation: Burgerbelangen opposes the dog tax. However, it currently generates income. This means we would have to find that money elsewhere, for example through further cuts or increasing the ozb. We are not in favor of that. At this time, the dog tax is cost-neutral.

122526.explanation: In recent years, Rheden has asked a lot from residents by increasing the ozb. It is only fair that tourists also contribute. Therefore, Burgerbelangen opposes lowering the tourist tax.

122529.explanation: For preserving one’s own social network, we find it important that our own residents have priority. Refugees must be housed, but not at the expense of our own residents.

122530.explanation: If this were financially possible, we would like to do it, but at this moment it is not realistic. Moreover, we do not find it unreasonable that people who bring more waste also pay more.

122532.explanation: If municipalities were to receive funds for this, we would gladly reduce it. But at the moment, it is not affordable for Rheden and therefore not realistic.

122533.explanation: Under strict conditions, for example regarding nuisance, we would accept a coffeeshop. However, it must not lead to nuisance in the neighborhood.

122534.explanation: Regionally, we currently apply a 30% social rental housing rate. We find this sufficient. Raising the percentage to 40% makes many projects unprofitable. This ultimately only leads to less construction.

122535.explanation: Building upwards is inevitable to solve the housing shortage. We must carefully consider where and how tall we allow buildings. In a project like the IJsseldistrict in Velp, high-rise buildings can fit well in the surroundings.

122537.explanation: We are not against a merger with Brummen. Brummen resembles Rheden: a few large centers with smaller villages around them. Nature and agricultural landscape between them. A merger must not be at Rheden’s expense. No irresponsible (financial) risks. Cooperation with the Arnhem region is desirable.

122539.explanation: We need our business parks to accommodate companies where our residents can work. It cannot be that companies (and jobs) cannot find space because our business parks are occupied by associations mainly with members from outside Rheden.

122486.explanation: We find a balanced distribution important, but it is not leading.

122487.explanation: Provided the topic lends itself to it and the choice can easily be reduced to a for or against position.

122490.explanation: We support providing farmers with additional income opportunities, but the environment must be taken into account.

122493.explanation: We see it as positive that recognized refugees are already housed in an asylum seekers’ center in our municipality. At the same time, we must keep an eye on the needs of all residents.

122494.explanation: We support good housing, but this does not necessarily have to be on the farmers’ own business land.

122498.explanation: Difficult to organize so that everyone in all villages can enjoy it.

122500.explanation: The municipality can provide financial contributions where the entrepreneur cannot fully pay themselves.

122506.explanation: As a municipality, you can look at how to support in other ways (e.g., guaranteeing a loan).

122508.explanation: At this moment, we see no reason for this.

122509.explanation: At this moment, we see no reason for this.

122511.explanation: If the municipality can financially afford it.

122512.explanation: It must be prevented that people from our municipality are no longer able to find housing in other municipalities because of this arrangement.

122517.explanation: We currently see no reason for this.

122518.explanation: We see no reason to change this.

122521.explanation: On many roads in the villages, we support 30 km/h, but we want to maintain 50 km/h on the through roads.

122522.explanation: It is nice if that is possible, but it is not a given.

122523.explanation: Quality is at least as important.

122524.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has increased a lot in recent years and, in our opinion, could be lowered again.

122525.explanation: Provided that sufficient other income streams are available.

122526.explanation: We are in favor of keeping it as it is now, so no increase either.

122529.explanation: There must be a fair distribution.

122530.explanation: At this moment, we see no reason for this.

122532.explanation: We prefer supporting those children who do not have the opportunity to take swimming lessons.

122533.explanation: This does not fit with a safe and healthy municipality.

122534.explanation: As CDA, we support at least 30% social rental housing.

122535.explanation: We want to preserve the village-like green character of the municipality. High residential towers do not belong here.

122537.explanation: In our view, this does offer possibilities.

122539.explanation: If the club can pay a market-conform price for it.

122486.explanation: The most important thing is that the best people are in the right position. It does not matter whether they are men or women.

122487.explanation: We trust in the strength of our residents. When faced with a major decision, the residents of Rheden deserve a referendum, not a decision that is rushed through or made behind closed doors.

122490.explanation: Farmers are already struggling with all environmental measures and restrictions and are facing increasing difficulties. They must be given the space to keep their operations profitable, while taking the environment and livability into account.

122493.explanation: We do not want an asylum seekers' center (AZC) at all. There are already two asylum reception centers in the municipality. That is two too many. We will continue to strongly oppose this policy. Currently, more than 400 asylum seekers are being accommodated, which is already 150 more than legally required. This number must be reduced.

122494.explanation: In Velp there are several locations close together where large groups of labor migrants live communally. This has negative effects on safety and livability of the area. Employers must ensure proper housing on their own premises, even after dismissal.

122496.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves are part of life in our villages. Restricting or banning them is nonsensical and goes too far. The municipality should focus on addressing real nuisance, not on limiting normal and responsible use by residents.

122498.explanation: A municipal fireworks show sounds nice, but costs a lot of money and will not automatically solve the nuisance around New Year's Eve. The municipality must first focus on safety, enforcement, and clear rules. A show can only be considered if it is affordable and truly adds value for residents.

122500.explanation: Accessibility is important, but financial support from the municipality should not be automatic. Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for having an accessible building. The municipality can support with clear rules, advice, and customized solutions but not broad subsidy schemes.

122506.explanation: The municipality must help sports clubs if problems arise through no fault of their own. No blank checks, but support to keep our villages vital.

122508.explanation: Rheden deserves visible enforcement. We will strengthen the community enforcement officer team so that every neighborhood and rural area remains safe and livable. For example, enforce against nuisance, pollution, and overhanging greenery. No fuss, just act.

122509.explanation: Nuisance, vandalism, and incidents concentrate in several recognizable locations: stations, shopping areas, and parking places. Residents and entrepreneurs have long indicated that they do not always feel safe there. Therefore, more camera surveillance at places where structural nuisance occurs.

122511.explanation: We would like this but it is not realistic. Rheden did have a starter loan, but it ended in 2010 because the national and provincial governments stopped co-funding. The municipality cannot bear these costs alone.

122512.explanation: Those who live, work, and contribute in Rheden should not be sidelined. We choose to genuinely prioritize people with local roots for social rental housing so our community does not grow apart.

122517.explanation: Exemption from municipal taxes is an essential instrument to combat poverty. It prevents debts, protects vulnerable households, and contributes to socially just local policy.

122518.explanation: If the income limit is raised, aid for those hardest hit becomes diluted. Resources must be deployed where need is greatest, not spread more broadly. It must be prevented that schemes become unaffordable and support fragmented.

122520.explanation: We want to meet growing demand for charging infrastructure and a balanced distribution of charging points in all villages. By timely investment in sufficient capacity, public spaces remain accessible and future-proof.

122521.explanation: The maximum speed within built-up areas should be 30 km/h where necessary for safety, but 50 km/h should remain where it can be justified. This way, we keep our villages safe for pedestrians and cyclists without unnecessarily delaying residents who are on the move.

122522.explanation: No! Everyone must adapt to Dutch values and norms. Choosing healthcare providers based on religion, culture, or beliefs conflicts with this. Healthcare is equal for everyone, and we adhere to shared Dutch values instead of separate systems alongside each other.

122523.explanation: Cheap is often expensive. Price must never be the primary criterion when purchasing youth care. The municipality must focus on quality, expertise, and effectiveness. Too strong a focus on costs leads to fragmentation, provider changes, and ultimately higher societal costs.

122524.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has risen by more than 75% in four years. Municipal services have not improved to the same extent. Deficits should not always be covered by tax increases but first absorbed within the organization. A lower OZB ensures a fairer tax level.

122525.explanation: Dog tax is outdated. It taxes one group of residents without clear reason. Dogs also serve a social function (especially for the elderly). Dogs combat loneliness, benefit physical and mental health, and promote social contacts and friendships.

122526.explanation: Tourist tax does not need to be lowered. It is a fair contribution from visitors for the facilities they use. With this income, the municipality can invest in maintenance and livability without increasing the burden on residents.

122529.explanation: Priority for status holders undermines trust in society and government. People often wait many years on a waiting list and see newcomers get housing with priority. Status holders must join the queue and wait their turn.

122530.explanation: Those who dispose of waste neatly should not be penalized with extra costs. Free disposal is logical, effective, and prevents litter in public spaces.

122532.explanation: Swimming safety is important. It prevents accidents, reduces inequality, and saves societal costs in the long term. The municipality must financially contribute to school swimming so every child can learn to swim.

122533.explanation: A coffeeshop does not belong in our villages. It causes nuisance and harms the living environment.

122534.explanation: 40% is too high. A maximum of 30% social rental prevents unbalanced growth. Rheden also needs affordable owner-occupied and mid-range rental housing, otherwise the housing market will stall.

122535.explanation: Not necessarily. In Rheden, we build for our residents, but without losing the human scale. High towers are not a given. Housing must remain affordable, but not at the expense of the village character.

122537.explanation: Rheden can still manage, but it is showing signs of strain. If we do nothing, we will lose control. By joining forces with Brummen, we strengthen the organization and maintain service levels. We do want our residents to be closely involved, preferably through a referendum.

122539.explanation: The Velp model building club should have a chance to remain at the De Beemd business park. This club is a place where technology, creativity, and community come together. It deserves stability, not uncertainty about the future.

122486.explanation: A reliable government is recognizable and representative. Therefore, it is good to pay attention to diversity within the municipal council, the executive board, and the civil service, for example in terms of age, gender, disabilities, background, and education level.

122487.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. A (broad) survey is a better alternative in cases where the municipality wants to involve residents' opinions. Local government remains ultimately responsible.

122490.explanation: We want to give farmers space to build a few homes on their farms or to realize other (recreational) activities. We also want to make it easier to apply diversified farming, such as care farming and farm shops.

122493.explanation: Every municipality has a responsibility in asylum seeker reception to ensure nationwide distribution, and we are working on this. We support small-scale reception. We will consult with social organizations on how best to organize this.

122494.explanation: The municipality strictly enforces good living conditions. We cooperate with inspection services and social organizations to identify and address abuses by employers and landlords.

122496.explanation: The Christian Union commits to phasing out wood burning in the coming years while simultaneously ensuring that residents who use wood burning for home heating do not end up in the cold. Good transition arrangements and more air quality measurements are needed.

122498.explanation: Nothing recorded in the election program. In principle, out of care for creation, we want to prevent nuisance and damage to nature; at the same time, such events contribute to solidarity and community spirit and are safer than letting off fireworks oneself.

122500.explanation: This is a responsibility of the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality's role is to redesign public spaces to improve accessibility for multiple groups, including children, youth, elderly, and people with disabilities.

122506.explanation: Sports clubs often act as the glue of society and deserve support. We stimulate grassroots sports in the municipality of Rheden; sport should be available to everyone. Therefore, it is necessary to have sufficient sports facilities for the future, and we see our role there.

122508.explanation: We strengthen cooperation between retailers, enforcement officers, and police to tackle shoplifting and disturbances. We do this concretely through regular consultations between retailers' associations in the villages and government services.

122509.explanation: The Christian Union stands for a resilient society as the best preparation in an unstable world. In the growing concerns about safety due to globalization, the solution is not 'every person for themselves', but a society where people care for one another can make a difference.

122511.explanation: We especially want to focus on sufficient affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for starters, others with limited means, and people with care needs. In new construction projects with owner-occupied homes, we ask for attention to offerings up to €300,000.

122512.explanation: We follow the allocation rules agreed upon in the Arnhem-Nijmegen residential region with neighboring municipalities and housing corporations.

122517.explanation: The municipality of Rheden applies a social cancellation and collection policy when collecting municipal taxes and levies to prevent problematic debts.

122518.explanation: We continue to actively cooperate with housing corporations, energy companies, and health insurers to detect payment arrears early and offer help. The municipality continues to cooperate with voluntary debt assistance organizations and contributes sufficiently financially.

122520.explanation: The number of charging stations will be expanded in accordance with the NAL standard. Residents will have the option to charge their own car via a cable over the sidewalk or a small groove between the tiles, provided sufficient precautions are taken.

122521.explanation: Only on roads with public transport and main connecting roads shall 50 kilometers per hour apply where possible. This is only possible when there are separate bicycle paths.

122522.explanation: We are therefore committed to ensuring that people in Rheden also look out for each other. This can only happen if people know each other and feel involved. The municipality can provide what is needed to get to know each other and facilitate initiatives from residents and organizations.

122523.explanation: An important challenge for the municipality of Rheden is to ensure that youth care remains accessible for vulnerable children and youth. According to the Christian Union, this requires first and foremost investment in families and communities, including support for relationship problems.

122524.explanation: Nothing realistic given the current budgets and pressure on municipal tasks.

122525.explanation: Nothing included in the election program.

122526.explanation: Nothing included in the election program.

122529.explanation: The municipality has a responsibility to ensure that status holders can participate in society. Having their own place is essential. Integration works best if people can fully participate in society as early as possible.

122530.explanation: Residents can offer bulky waste free of charge at least once a year, collected on demand by the claw truck, with a maximum of 3 m3 per time. Disposal at the Recycling Center must remain affordable for all residents, with special attention to low-income households.

122532.explanation: The Christian Union invests in families and communities, among other things by continuing to allocate budgets for the Gelrepas and Stichting Leergeld.

122533.explanation: The Christian Union opposes the presence of coffee shops and/or the cultivation of cannabis by municipalities. Drug use should no longer be normalized. The municipality will do more to combat the increasing use and growing acceptance of synthetic drugs.

122534.explanation: We build in all price ranges. This ensures a healthy mix of affordable and more expensive homes in all our neighborhoods and village centers. We choose for housing projects a distribution of two-thirds of all newly built homes in the affordable segment, of which 30% social rental.

122535.explanation: We are not deterred by housing projects with high-rise buildings if they can be suitably integrated into village centers and the environment and contribute to the aforementioned goals.

122537.explanation: Rheden is larger than Brummen, but we think we will have the same problem in a few years. That is why we want to merge. Together with the municipality of Brummen, we are one municipality with many villages. Local communities are where it happens, and we must keep them strong.

122539.explanation: We believe the model building club cannot stay at De Beemd because the site needs to be used for companies in a higher environmental category. At the same time, we cherish associations and an alternative must be made available for the model building club.

122486.explanation: An inclusive executive board is a nice goal, but not mandatory upfront. Knowledge and experience are just as important.

122487.explanation: Participation is highly desired and should be well guided, leaving decision-making to the council.

122490.explanation: As long as it remains small-scale and primarily sells own products, that is a fine idea.

122493.explanation: The municipality of Rheden already does enough for asylum seeker reception relative to the number of residents. Now first build new senior housing for the much-needed flow-through.

122494.explanation: All housing falls under the Environmental Planning Act. It is undesirable to make seasonal workers dependent on these employers.

122496.explanation: Much nuisance can be prevented with good information. Do not create complex regulations that enforcement must check behind closed doors.

122498.explanation: The fireworks ban goes into effect next year. We'll wait and see how residents spend the evening.

122500.explanation: Yes, for its own municipal buildings (also during renovations). Entrepreneurs will have to invest themselves. The municipality can, however, invest in more public toilets in town centers.

122506.explanation: An association has members. They are the first to be called upon to save their association. An exception can always be made for very exceptional circumstances.

122508.explanation: Safety on the street (and online) is very important. However, ensure that the enforcement officers are actually on the street and not behind a laptop.

122509.explanation: Especially in the town centers, to prevent fraud targeting the elderly.

122511.explanation: The municipality is not a mortgage bank. Shoemaker, stick to your last.

122512.explanation: In new construction, require 10% senior housing in social rental for elderly local housing seekers. Also 10% senior housing in planned home ownership. This creates flow (ultimately benefiting the youth).

122517.explanation: Forgiving debts does not help someone in the long term. When people receive financial aid, it should be linked to budgeting courses. However, avoid fines making taxes much higher.

122518.explanation: This is mostly national policy, which the municipality should follow.

122520.explanation: This involves monitoring developments in the Dutch vehicle fleet.

122521.explanation: This especially applies to the town centers themselves, so that the elderly can cross safely. Whether the entire built-up area should be the boundary is still to be discussed.

122523.explanation: Millions of euros flow toward youth care in Rheden. The council’s attention should focus more on effective spending of funds, even (or especially) if the financing is done regionally (new law).

122524.explanation: 50PLUS wants to advocate for (local) taxes that do not rise more than the indexation of AOW and/or pensions. For Rheden, this primarily means a reduction of the property tax (OZB) in the coming four years.

122525.explanation: Provided that abolishing it also entails spending cuts and stricter enforcement of loose dogs that pose a danger to the elderly.

122526.explanation: The municipality of Rheden is popular with tourists. Keep the tax level in line with national developments in busy areas.

122529.explanation: The urgency (priority) for social rental housing is already arranged. Build a lot of new homes and fairly distribute them among all housing seekers. Do not make extra exceptions.

122530.explanation: Returning items should be linked to an identity document.

122532.explanation: Swimming lessons are of great importance for everyone.

122533.explanation: As long as drugs are not fully legal (supply and sale), their use should not be made easier through coffee shops.

122534.explanation: 30% is sufficient, of which 10% are lifetime-adapted homes for the elderly.

122535.explanation: Not throughout all of Rheden and certainly no skyscrapers. What can be built where is arranged in the Spatial Plan. There is room for public participation on this.

122537.explanation: Provided it results in lower per capita taxes from the start and information points return to village centers, for example once a week.

122539.explanation: The land belongs to the municipality and can be sold (highest yield desired for the budget). It is legally complex beyond that, and we prefer to leave it to the courts.

122486.explanation: Different groups of people must be well represented. In the new council, we aim for a diverse, inclusive composition.

122487.explanation: We ensure that residents have influence on important decisions. With advisory referenda and neighborhood referenda, we give everyone in the municipality a voice.

122490.explanation: A farm shop or campsite offers new opportunities for a retiring livestock farmer. Together we look at what fits in the environment, with protection of nature and tranquility.

122493.explanation: We welcome refugees. Rheden takes its responsibility. We choose small-scale and humane shelter that helps asylum seekers integrate more quickly.

122494.explanation: We want fair housing for migrant workers. Employers must take responsibility, but housing on their own premises makes migrant workers too dependent on their employer.

122496.explanation: We want clean and healthy air in Rheden. Wood burning causes fine particulate matter and health damage, so we are phasing out the use of fireplaces and wood stoves.

122498.explanation: We ensure a New Year without fear and pollution. No fireworks at events, but creative and animal-friendly alternatives, such as a drone show.

122500.explanation: We make accessibility the norm. New buildings must be suitable for everyone, and we address existing buildings as a municipality role model, without subsidies for entrepreneurs.

122506.explanation: We choose a strong community life. That is why the municipality supports sports clubs when needed.

122508.explanation: Rheden invests in more street workers, neighborhood parents, enforcement officers, and district police officers, the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. Thanks to their efforts, possible problems can be identified early before they escalate.

122509.explanation: We do not choose more camera surveillance. Cameras often give a false sense of security and infringe on the privacy of Rheden residents.

122511.explanation: We want to help starters because housing is a basic right. But the starter loan is not the right instrument, as it drives up prices and saddles people with high debts. The municipality should specifically build affordable homes for this group.

122512.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing. We need extra attention for those who need it most, such as the homeless. Excluding people based on where they lived before only worsens the housing shortage.

122517.explanation: We want to spare people with low incomes. We expand the criteria for exemption: households up to 130% of the social minimum are entitled to exemption from municipal levies.

122518.explanation: No one should have to stretch their budget each month with the stress of wondering if there is enough for groceries. Therefore, we raise the support threshold to 130% of the social minimum. This gives more households financial breathing room.

122520.explanation: We want to make the switch to clean transport easier. Therefore, we provide sufficient fast-charging points powered by green electricity for private and shared cars.

122521.explanation: We opt for safe streets and clean air. Therefore, we make 30 km/h the norm in built-up areas (and 15 km/h in residential zones and near schools), and design streets accordingly.

122522.explanation: We choose care that fits the person. Where reasonable, caregivers take into account faith, culture, and identity.

122523.explanation: We want young people to really move forward through the help they receive. That is why we focus primarily on quality in youth care rather than costs.

122524.explanation: Rheden continues to invest in people, animals, and nature. A property tax (OZB) reduction would undermine these ambitions.

122525.explanation: We want to abolish the unfair dog tax. Until then, we will use the proceeds only for good dog facilities.

122526.explanation: We choose to use the tourist tax for sustainability. A reduction does not lead to more overnight stays, but does mean less money for nature and the living environment.

122529.explanation: We want to help refugees with a residence permit find housing. To do this and to accommodate residents of Rheden, we want to build more affordable homes and combat vacancy.

122530.explanation: We want less waste and more reuse. That's why we keep the collection of 1 m³ bulky waste free and encourage repair cafes, thrift stores, and reuse.

122532.explanation: We want every child to learn to swim safely. With school swimming, we ensure equal opportunities and safety.

122533.explanation: We opt for a health-oriented approach. We do not prohibit a legal and well-regulated coffeeshop, but we remain alert to the risks.

122534.explanation: We ensure that living remains affordable. With firm agreements, we ensure that at least 40% of new homes are social rental and that this remains so.

122535.explanation: We choose buildings that fit our villages and the landscape. High-rise flats disrupt that and we do not allow them.

122537.explanation: Such a significant decision must be submitted to the residents. She advocates for an advisory referendum in case of a possible municipal redivision, so that the population participates in the decision.

122539.explanation: We choose to give space to associations and social initiatives. The model building club simply belongs there.

122486.explanation: If this is not the norm, men will remain overrepresented and women underrepresented in public administration. Moreover, this creates a different dynamic in the municipal executive and leads to better decisions.

122487.explanation: Volt is in favor of a citizens' assembly where residents are involved in plans beforehand. Volt is against Yes/No consultations afterwards.

122490.explanation: Preferably yes, but not always. Sometimes there are reasons to deny permission, for example if the land is part of a Natura2000 area.

122493.explanation: Volt supports the distribution law to share burdens more fairly, so Rheden must do its part.

122494.explanation: Every employer must take good care of their employees. If they have seasonal workers coming from far away, this means they must provide appropriate housing at cost price, regardless of whether this is on their own company premises.

122496.explanation: There are people who experience health complaints from burning wood. At the same time, there are people who depend on wood burning or derive well-being from it. Compliance with the burning guide is a first step.

122498.explanation: As with any behavioral change, it is necessary to offer an alternative: to entice residents to no longer buy and set off fireworks themselves, an alternative is desired.

122500.explanation: Volt is for an inclusive society. Building standards should take more account of people with disabilities. As long as this is not yet arranged, the municipality can provide financial support.

122506.explanation: Every sports association is responsible for good financial management. The municipality can facilitate and support.

122508.explanation: Residents perceive the municipality as relatively safe (compared to national figures), so we can better spend our money elsewhere.

122509.explanation: Residents perceive the municipality as relatively safe (compared to national figures), so we can better spend our money elsewhere.

122511.explanation: Such measures drive up prices. The municipality is also not a lender; this is not its responsibility. It might possibly act as a guarantor.

122512.explanation: There are many factors that together determine the urgency of housing seekers. Volt wants to look at this in a nuanced way.

122517.explanation: Sometimes forgiving debts is more effective in helping people than enforcement. This must be assessed on a case-by-case basis.

122518.explanation: Volt currently has too little information to take a position on this. Volt supports a basic income.

122520.explanation: The municipality must do everything possible to accelerate the energy transition efficiently.

122521.explanation: This has a proven effect on traffic deaths and casualties, especially among children and cyclists. Let's take the facts seriously.

122522.explanation: That would be nice, but such individualistic choices are not always possible and/or affordable. Quality of care is most important.

122523.explanation: Besides price, expertise, effectiveness, and quality are at least as important.

122524.explanation: The municipality of Rheden is at the national average. Lowering the property tax creates a financing problem.

122525.explanation: The municipality of Rheden is at the national average. Abolishing the dog tax creates a financing problem. Substantively, we might even better consider a cat tax, given the environmental damage they cause.

122526.explanation: The municipality of Rheden is at the national average. Lowering the tourist tax creates a financing problem.

122529.explanation: This has already been determined nationally.

122530.explanation: This is useful to prevent illegal dumping, especially in nature.

122532.explanation: In recent years, school swimming has disappeared and the number of children who have drowned has risen... Every child in the Netherlands must be able to swim.

122533.explanation: According to Volt, the sale of soft drugs should be regarded like the sale of alcohol. Both are addictive and equally harmful to health.

122534.explanation: Given the number of housing seekers in the social rental sector, this must be at least 33 percent.

122535.explanation: Except for transforming existing high industrial and office buildings into homes.

122537.explanation: Volt has too little information to take a position on this. The main question Volt does not see answered: why must the municipality of Brummen merge as a whole? Why isn’t the option of splitting up on the table?

122539.explanation: This association causes no nuisance to anyone. They have been there so long that they have acquired certain rights. Terminating the lease effectively means dissolving the club and thus ending an association and national cultural heritage.

statements.title: The municipality of Rheden should merge with the municipality of Brummen.

titleParts.glossary: The merging of two or more municipalities.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Rheden should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain allowed in Rheden.

titleParts.text: In Rheden moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Rheden should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Rheden should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Rheden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: High-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Rheden.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be lowered.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: At least 40% of the newly built homes in Rheden should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Rheden moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The Velp model-building club should be able to remain at the De Beemd business park.
